item.title: JavaScript 是如何工作的？？？ | How Does JavaScript Work ???
item.description:
简而言之，JavaScript 在单线程环境中借助事件循环异步工作，以在浏览器中不间断地运行。

现在要更深入地解释它。

基本上，JavaScript 在解释器的帮助下在浏览器中运行，解释器根据给定的顺序解释每一行 JavaScript 代码并在浏览器中执行它。JavaScript 是一种高级、动态和解释型编程语言，广泛用于构建 Web应用程序虽然它有广泛的使用领域。 当用户与页面交互时，JavaScript 响应诸如单击按钮、更新 DOM 和执行代码等事件。代码在 JavaScript 引擎中执行，该引擎主要与浏览器一起构建，解析并运行代码。 还可以发出网络请求以从服务器获取数据，从而允许在页面上显示动态和最新信息。

什么是 JavaScript 事件循环？ 想听听我的故事吗？

从前，有一个繁忙的小镇，叫做 JavaScript Land。 镇上总是熙熙攘攘，居民们忙于他们的日常工作。 一天，JavaScript Land 镇长发现小镇发展迅速，需要完成的任务每天都在增加。

为了跟上需求，市长决定创建一个新系统来管理镇上的所有任务。 他称之为“事件循环”。 当一个任务完成时，事件循环将移至队列中的下一个任务。

起初，在 JavaScript Land 中一切顺利。 然而随着时间的推移，队列中加入的任务越来越多。

事件循环开始陷入困境，镇民们变得很沮丧。 有些任务比他们应该花的时间要长得多，而其他任务则被搁置和遗忘。

他决定对某些任务进行优先排序，以便先完成最重要的任务。 这样，镇民们就可以尽快回到他们的日常工作中。

于是，Event Loop 得到了改进，JavaScript Land 再次顺利运行。 镇民们从此过上了幸福的生活。

那么，您是否了解这个故事的主题事件循环是一种机制，它可以帮助 JavaScript 以异步方式一次解释一段代码，以便在浏览器中顺利运行。

现在让我们用一个新故事来解释这里的异步术语 ::)))

当然，让我们考虑一个真实的场景来理解 JavaScript 中的异步处理。

想象一下，您在一家快餐店点了汉堡和薯条。 您必须等待食物准备好，但您不想在等待期间站在那里什么都不做。 因此，您决定利用这段时间做些其他事情，例如浏览手机或读书。

这是现实生活中异步处理的经典例子。 在等待食物准备好的同时，您可以做其他事情，当食物准备好时您会收到通知。

同样，JavaScript 中的异步处理允许您在后台执行长时间运行的任务，而不会阻塞执行的主线程。 因此，您的网站或应用程序可以继续响应用户交互，即使在后台处理任务时也是如此。

这使您的网站或应用程序响应更快、效率更高，并且您的用户不必等待长时间运行的任务完成就可以继续使用您的网站或应用程序。

这就是今天的全部内容，您会看到 JavaScript 如何发展以在这个数字世界中为我们提供更高效和良好的用户体验，因为他知道时间很宝贵，因此他节省了大量时间来帮助网站提高效率。



原文:

In a short JavaScript work asynchronously with help of event loop in a single threaded environment to run non stop in the browser .

Now going To explain it more deeper .

﻿Basically JavaScript run in the browser with the help of a interpreter which interprets each line of JavaScript code according to the given order and execute it in the browser .JavaScript is a high level , dynamic and interpreted programming language which is widely used for building web apps though it has a wide range of using field. JavaScript can interact with the DOM (document object model ) to dynamically modify the content , structure and styles of the page . When a user interact with the page , JavaScript respond to events such as clicking on a button and updating the DOM and executing code .The code is executed in JavaScript engine which are mostly in-build with the browsers, which parses and runs the code . JavaScript
can also make network requests to fetch data from a server allowing for dynamic and up to date information to be displayed on the page .

what is JavaScript event loop? want to here a story from me ?

Once upon a time, there was a busy little town called JavaScript Land. The town was always bustling with activity, as the inhabitants went about their daily tasks. One day, the mayor of JavaScript Land realized that the town was growing rapidly, and the number of tasks that needed to be completed was increasing every day.

To keep up with the demand, the mayor decided to create a new system for managing all the tasks in the town. He called it the "Event Loop." The Event Loop was designed to handle all the tasks that needed to be done in the town, one by one.

Each task in JavaScript Land was placed in a queue, and the Event Loop would work its way through the queue, completing each task in turn. When a task was completed, the Event Loop would move on to the next task in the queue.

At first, things ran smoothly in JavaScript Land. The Event Loop worked efficiently, and the inhabitants were happy with the new system. However, as time went by, more and more tasks were added to the queue.

The Event Loop started to get bogged down, and the townspeople grew frustrated. Some tasks were taking much longer to complete than they should have, and others were being pushed aside and forgotten.

The mayor of JavaScript Land realized that he needed to find a solution to the problem. He decided to prioritize certain tasks, so that the most important ones would be completed first. This way, the townspeople could get back to their daily tasks as quickly as possible.

And so, the Event Loop was improved, and JavaScript Land ran smoothly once again. The Event Loop continued to handle all the tasks in the town, one by one, making sure that the most important tasks were completed first. And the townspeople lived happily ever after.

So , did you understand the main theme of this story event loop is mechanism which helps JavaScript to interpret one piece of code at a time in an asynchronous way to run smoothly in the browser.

Now lets explain the asynchronous term here with a new story :::)))﻿

Sure, let's consider a real-life scenario to understand asynchronous processing in JavaScript.

Imagine you're at a fast-food restaurant and you place an order for a burger and fries. You have to wait for your food to be prepared, but you don't want to stand around and do nothing while you wait. So, you decide to use the time to do something else, like browsing your phone or reading a book.

This is a classic example of asynchronous processing in real life. While you wait for your food to be prepared, you can do other things, and you'll be notified when your food is ready.

In the same way, asynchronous processing in JavaScript allows you to carry out long-running tasks in the background, without blocking the main thread of execution. So, your website or application can continue to respond to user interactions, even while a task is being processed in the background.

This makes your website or application more responsive and efficient, and your users won't have to wait for long-running tasks to complete before they can continue using your website or application.

That is all for today, you see how JavaScript had evolved to serve us more efficient and good user experience in this digital world as he knows time is valuable, so he saves a lot of time helping the website to be more efficient.
item.author: mahlilmahee

item.title: 什么是C#中的接口和抽象类及其区别？ | what is an interface and abstract class in c# and its difference?
item.description:
将界面想象成一个超级严格的老板，他向他的员工（类）列出了任务列表，并期望他们完美地完成所有这些任务。

老板（界面）只知道需要做什么，但不知道应该怎么做。 因此，他将工作描述（方法、属性和事件的列表）交给他的员工（班级），并说“完成它，我不在乎怎么做，只要确保你按照说明进行操作即可。”

而这正是类所做的！ 他们从老板（界面）那里获取职位描述，然后想出自己的方法来完成任务。 老板只要完成所有任务就开心，员工也开心，因为他们可以发挥自己的创造力来解决问题。

现在，让我们谈谈抽象类。 想象一个抽象类作为一个老板，他更多地参与到这个过程中。 他给他的员工（班级）一份任务清单，但也提供了一些关于如何完成这些任务的指导。 他说：“我对应该如何完成这些任务有一个大致的想法，但请随意发挥自己的创造力并提出自己的解决方案。”

接口和抽象类之间的主要区别在于，接口仅定义需要做什么，而抽象类则提供做什么和怎么做。

因此，总而言之，接口就像老板将任务委派给他的员工，但不知道应该如何完成。 而班级就像是员工，按照自己的方式完成任务。

在技​​术方面：-

界面
它定义了一组属性、方法和事件，这些属性、方法和事件必须由实现该接口的任何类实现。 接口提供了一种方法来定义类与其客户之间的契约。 客户端可以使用此契约与类的对象进行交互，而无需了解底层实现。

接口仅包含方法、属性和事件的签名，但不包含实现。 接口成员的实现由实现该接口的类提供。

接口对于创建松散耦合的系统很有用，因为可以更改接口的实现而不影响依赖于该接口的代码。 随着时间的推移，这使得维护和发展系统变得更加容易。

 interface <interface_name> { // properties, methods, and events }

类使用“implements”关键字实现接口：

 class MyClass : <interface_name> { // implementation of properties, methods, and events }

抽象类
抽象类提供可由多个派生类共享的属性、方法和事件的基本实现。

抽象类可以同时包含抽象成员和非抽象成员（方法和属性）。 抽象成员使用“abstract”关键字声明并且没有主体。 派生类必须实现这些抽象成员，提供它们自己的实现。

当你想为多个类提供一个公共的基础实现，但仍然允许类之间的实现有一些变化时，抽象类很有用。

 abstract class <abstract_class_name> { // abstract and non-abstract members }

从抽象类派生的类使用“：”运算符：

 class MyClass : <abstract_class_name> { // implementation of abstract members and additional members }

总之，接口和抽象类是 C# 中的重要概念，它们提供了一种定义契约和创建可重用代码的方法。 接口用于定义类必须实现的一组方法和属性，而抽象类提供可由多个派生类共享的基本实现。

在接口和抽象类之间进行选择取决于项目的特定需求以及所涉及的类之间的关系。 当你想为多个类定义一个契约来实现时，最好使用接口，而当你想提供一个可以被多个派生类共享的公共基础实现时，最好使用抽象类。

了解接口和抽象类之间的区别，以及何时以及如何有效地使用它们，对于编写可维护、可伸缩和高效的代码来说是很重要的。

谢谢你的时间。
祝你有美好的一天。



原文:

Think of an interface as a super strict boss who gives a list of tasks to his employees (classes) and expects them to complete all of those tasks perfectly.

The boss (interface) only knows what needs to be done, but he has no idea how it should be done. So, he hands off the job description (a list of methods, properties, and events) to his employees (classes) and says "Get it done, I don't care how, just make sure you follow the instructions."

And that's exactly what classes do! They take the job description from the boss (interface), and they come up with their own ways of completing the tasks. The boss is happy as long as all the tasks are completed, and the employees are happy because they get to use their own creativity to solve the problems.

Now, let's talk about abstract classes. Imagine an abstract class as a boss who is a bit more involved in the process. He gives his employees (classes) a list of tasks to do, but also provides some guidance on how to do those tasks. He says "I have a general idea of how these tasks should be done, but feel free to use your own creativity and come up with your own solution."

The key difference between an interface and an abstract class is that an interface only defines what needs to be done, whereas an abstract class provides both the what and the how.

So, to summarize, an interface is like a boss who delegates tasks to his employees, but has no idea how they should be done. And classes are like employees who complete the tasks according to their own methods.

In technological terms:-

INTERFACE
An interface in C# is a blueprint for a class. It defines a set of properties, methods, and events that must be implemented by any class that implements the interface. Interfaces provide a way to define a contract between a class and its clients. The clients can use this contract to interact with objects of the class without needing to know the underlying implementation.

An interface contains only the signatures of methods, properties, and events, but not the implementation. The implementation of the members of an interface is provided by the classes that implement the interface.

Interfaces are useful for creating loosely-coupled systems, as the implementation of an interface can be changed without affecting the code that depends on the interface. This makes it easier to maintain and evolve the system over time.

interface <interface_name> 
{
  // properties, methods, and events
}

A class implements an interface using the "implements" keyword:

class MyClass : <interface_name>
{
  // implementation of properties, methods, and events
}

ABSTRACT CLASS
An abstract class in C# is a class that cannot be instantiated on its own, but must be derived from by another class. An abstract class provides a base implementation of properties, methods, and events that can be shared by multiple derived classes.

An abstract class can contain both abstract and non-abstract members (methods and properties). Abstract members are declared with the "abstract" keyword and do not have a body. The derived classes must implement these abstract members, providing their own implementation.

Abstract classes are useful when you want to provide a common base implementation for multiple classes, but still allow for some variation in the implementation between the classes.

abstract class <abstract_class_name> 
{
  // abstract and non-abstract members
}

A class that derives from an abstract class uses the ":” operator:

class MyClass : <abstract_class_name>
{
  // implementation of abstract members and additional members
}

In conclusion, interfaces and abstract classes are important concepts in C# that provide a way to define contracts and create reusable code. Interfaces are used to define a set of methods and properties that a class must implement, while abstract classes provide a base implementation that can be shared by multiple derived classes.

Choosing between an interface and an abstract class depends on the specific needs of your project and the relationships between the classes involved. Interfaces are best used when you want to define a contract for multiple classes to implement, whereas abstract classes are best used when you want to provide a common base implementation that can be shared by multiple derived classes.

It's important to understand the differences between interfaces and abstract classes, as well as when and how to use them effectively, in order to write maintainable, scalable, and efficient code.

Thanks for you time.
have a good day.
item.author: santan47

item.title: Firebase 学习：要牢记的关键注意事项 | Firebase Learning: Key Considerations to Keep in Mind
item.description:
它提供可轻松集成到您的应用程序中的托管、身份验证、存储和数据库。 它最受欢迎的工具之一称为 Firestore，这是一种键值非关系数据库。 这意味着使用类似于 JSON 的结构来存储信息。

在我使用 Firebase 和 Google Cloud 的专业经验中，我遇到了优化和保护应用程序以及改进开发流程的挑战。 为了帮助其他人克服这些困难，我整理了一些我希望从一开始就知道的重要见解。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，本文概述的概念对于成功学习这些技术都是必不可少的。

安全规则

如果您没有在生产应用程序中正确实施安全规则，可能会产生严重后果。 如果没有适当的安全措施，任何人都可能通过 API 访问（例如通过 Bash 或 Powershell）修改您的数据库。

在编写任何代码之前设计您的 Firebase 安全规则至关重要。 如果在开发过程的最后添加安全规则，您可能会面临累积的技术债务和安全漏洞问题，解决这些问题既费时又费钱。

安全规则应被视为数据库设计的关键组成部分，并应在整个项目中进行讨论，尤其是在早期阶段。 修改和更改纸上草稿或 Jamboard 比追溯修复影响众多用户的生产实施更有效。

有多种模式可用于控制对应用程序的访问，包括：

  • 仅经过身份验证的访问
  • 仅限内容所有者访问
  • 粒度访问控制
  • 功能访问控制

仅经过身份验证的访问

您可以将阅读权限限制为仅登录用户的集合：

 rules_version = '2'; service cloud.firestore { match /databases/{database}/documents {  match posts/{document=**} {   allow read: if request.auth != null allow write: if false  } } }

仅限内容所有者访问

此外，您还可以实施仅限内容所有者的访问模式。 这样用户就只能访问自己的文档，即与用户uid相同的文档。

 rules_version = '2'; service cloud.firestore { match /databases/{database}/documents {  match /user/{userId}/{documents=**} {   allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId  } } }

粒度控制访问

另一种选择是添加更细化的规则以根据特定操作限制访问。

 rules_version = '2'; service cloud.firestore { match /databases/{database}/documents {  match /posts/{documents=**} {   allow read: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid && get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Reader" allow create, update: if request.auth != null if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Writer" allow delete: if request.auth != null if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Admin"  } } }

在此示例中，用户只有拥有阅读权限才能阅读帖子，只有作者才能创建和更新帖子。 此外，只有管理员可以删除posts集合中的内容。

功能控制访问

如果您的项目在您的安全规则中有很多重复代码，您可以将其重构为函数并调用这些函数，而不是复制和粘贴您的规则。

 rules_version = '2'; service cloud.firestore { match /databases/{database}/documents { function isAuthenticated(){ return request.auth != null } function isAdmin(){ return isAuthenticated() && get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Admin" }  match /products/{documents=**} { allow read, write: if isAdmin()  } } }

注意：您必须记住{documents=**}意味着它包括products集合中的所有子集合。

本地模拟器套件

如果 Firebase 的文档强调使用此工具的重要性，尤其是对于那些使用 Firebase Functions 的人来说，将会很有帮助。 但是，文档似乎并没有太多谈论它。 他们的网站上有与 Emulator Suite 相关的条目，但有时感觉有点乱。 我花了几个月的时间才明白我可以利用那个 Emulator Suite 以及如何正确使用它。

该工具可让您在自己的计算机上测试您的项目，而不会影响实际服务。 本地模拟器支持 Firestore、实时数据库、函数、存储、身份验证和 PubSub。

在将安全规则和存储规则发布到 Google Cloud 之前，使用本地模拟器测试它们可以帮助您及早发现问题。 此外，它还可以帮助您省钱，因为更少的部署意味着更少的费用。 将所有规则放在一个地方也可以更轻松地跟踪它们。

您可以查看官方文档以了解有关 Local Emulator Suite 的更多信息

性能和可扩展性优化

在小项目开始时关注可伸缩性和性能似乎是浪费时间。 在某种程度上，这是正确的。 工程师必须避免过度设计。 但是，对算法和逻辑有扎实的理解很重要，这样您就可以在项目增长和性能成为问题时做好准备。 否则，您可能最终不得不花费大量时间和金钱来修复缓慢的执行时间或高内存消耗。 为了提高效率，您应该学习编写更高效的 Firebase 函数的技术，例如如何删除大集合或更新具有复杂条件的许多文档。

如何删除大集合

一些集合可以增长到数百万个文档，删除其中的文档可能是一项计算密集型任务。 以下示例来自 Firebase 官方文档。 这些显示了处理此类任务的推荐方法。

 async function deleteCollection(db, collectionPath, batchSize) { const collectionRef = db.collection(collectionPath); const query = collectionRef.orderBy('__name__').limit(batchSize);  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {  deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve).catch(reject); }); } async function deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve) { const snapshot = await query.get();  const batchSize = snapshot.size; if (batchSize === 0) {  // When there are no documents left, we are done  resolve();  return; }  // Delete documents in a batch const batch = db.batch(); snapshot.docs.forEach((doc) => {  batch.delete(doc.ref); }); await batch.commit();  // Recurse on the next process tick, to avoid // exploding the stack. process.nextTick(() => {  deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve); }); }

如果您正在处理大文件并遇到处理问题，一种解决方案可能是为您的函数提供更多资源，例如额外的 RAM 或更长的超时时间。

 exports.convertLargeFile = functions  .runWith({   // Ensure the function has enough memory and time   // to process large files   timeoutSeconds: 300,   memory: "1GB",  })  .storage.object()  .onFinalize((object) => {   // Do some complicated things that take a lot of memory and time  })

排队的云任务

对于需要异步运行的耗时、资源密集型或带宽受限的任务，Google Cloud 提供了使用 Cloud Tasks 对函数进行排队的可能性。 您可以控制尝试次数、退避秒数和并发调度的最大值。 下面的例子来自官方文档：

 exports.backupApod = functions  .runWith( {secrets: ["SOME_KEY"]})  .tasks.taskQueue({   retryConfig: {    maxAttempts: 5,    minBackoffSeconds: 60,   },   rateLimits: {    maxConcurrentDispatches: 6,   },  }).onDispatch(async (data) => { //your complex task })
 exports.enqueueBackupTasks = functions.https.onRequest( async (_request, response) => { const queue = getFunctions().taskQueue("backupApod"); const enqueues = []; for (let i = 0; i <= 10; i += 1) {  // Enqueue each task with i*60 seconds day. Our task queue function  // should process ~1 task/min.  const scheduleDelaySeconds = i * 60  enqueues.push(    queue.enqueue(     { id: `task-${i}` },     {      scheduleDelaySeconds,      dispatchDeadlineSeconds: 60 * 5 // 5 minutes     },    ),  ); } await Promise.all(enqueues); response.sendStatus(200); });

据我所知，目前谷歌云没有办法自动备份 Firestore。 我使用BackupFire为我做这件事，但您也可以使用 Firebase Functions 编写解决方案。 请记住，当您恢复备份时， Firebase 会尝试将其与原始数据库合并，而不是覆盖它。 这可能会产生重要的后果。 考虑这个例子：

有两个客户：

  • 用户 A，uid： z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2
  • 用户 B，uid： Zk5olEuvZ1WokRrCqIN5gGaIu353

用户 A 获得了礼品卡或折扣代码，并将其保存在用户文档内的子集合中。

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discountCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

 { code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: false }

该应用程序允许用户将代码传输给他人。 用户A将密码转给用户B

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discountCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

 { code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true }

然而，由于紧急情况，不得不恢复前一天的备份。 经检查，数据库中存在不一致。 用户 A 和用户 B 现在都具有相同的折扣代码。

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

 { code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: false }

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

 { code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true }

为避免 Firestore 中的这些问题，您可以创建一个脚本来验证正确的文档并删除重复项。 另一种解决方案是以一种避免可以传输的文档的方式设计数据库，例如，使用数据库根路径中的集合。

discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

 { code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true createdBy: z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2 owner: Zk5olEuvZ1WokRrCqIN5gGaIu353 }

这样，只有一个代码可用，将其转移给另一个用户意味着只需编辑此文档中的所有者字段即可。 请记住，这是与 Firestore 的键值内部结构相关的特定问题，可能不会出现在其他数据库中。

结论

总之，在设计项目的早期阶段考虑可扩展性和性能效率很重要，尤其是使用 Firebase 时； 但没有过度工程化。 从长远来看，这可以节省时间和资源。 此外，必须为 Firestore 制定备份计划，因为恢复备份有时会导致不一致。 为了缓解这些问题，可以使用第三方服务或基于 Firebase Functions 创建自己的解决方案。 设计数据库的方式也很重要，以避免可能导致文档重复的可转移子集合。

参考书目

备用火 | 模拟器套件

安全规则基础

安全规则入门

谷歌开发者 | 任务功能

谷歌开发者 | 管理功能

谷歌云 | 2002 | 2002 | Delete a Firestore collection 删除 Firestore 集合



原文:

Firebase is a suite of cloud-based tools and services on Google Cloud Platform for application development. It offers hosting, authentication, storage, and databases that can be easily integrated into your app. One of its most popular tools is called Firestore, a key-value non-relational database. That means that uses structures similar to JSON to store information.

During my professional experience with Firebase and Google Cloud, I encountered challenges with optimizing and securing applications and improving the development process. To help others overcome these difficulties, I have compiled some key insights that I wish I had known from the start. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, the concepts outlined in this article are essential for a successful learning journey with these technologies.

Security Rules

Firebase documentation does not emphasize enough the importance of Security Rules, especially for those just starting to integrate Firebase into their projects. If you do not properly implement security rules in your production application it can have severe consequences. Without proper security, anyone can potentially modify your database through API access, such as through Bash or Powershell.

It is crucial to design your Firebase Security Rules before writing any code. If Security Rules are added at the end of the development process, you may be faced with the issue of accumulated technical debt and security vulnerabilities, which can be both time-consuming and costly to resolve.

Security Rules should be considered a critical component of database design and should be discussed throughout the entire project, especially in the early stages. It's more efficient to revise and make changes to a draft on paper or a Jamboard than to retroactively fix a production implementation affecting numerous users.

There are several patterns for controlling access to your application, including:

  • Authenticated-only access
  • Content-owner-only access
  • Granular access control
  • Functional access control

Authenticated-only access

You can restrict reading access to a collection of only signed-in users:

rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match posts/{document=**} {
      allow read: if request.auth != null
            allow write: if false
    }
  }
}

Content-owner-only access

Also, you can implement a content-owner-only access pattern. In this way, users can access only their own documents, that is, the documents that have the same uid as the user.

rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /user/{userId}/{documents=**} {
      allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId
    }
  }
}

Granular control access

Another option is to add more granular rules to restrict access depending on the specific operation.

rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /posts/{documents=**} {
      allow read: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid && get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Reader"
            allow create, update: if request.auth != null if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Writer"
            allow delete: if request.auth != null if get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Admin"
    }
  }
}

In this example, the user only can read a post if he has reading access, and only can create and update a post if he is a writer. Also, only an Admin can delete content inside the posts collection.

Functional control access

If your project has a lot of duplicated code in your security rules, you can refactor it into functions and call those functions instead of copy and paste your rules.

rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
        function isAuthenticated(){
            return request.auth != null
        }
        function isAdmin(){
            return isAuthenticated() && get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == "Admin"
        }

    match /products/{documents=**} {
            allow read, write: if isAdmin()
    }
  }
}

Note: You have to keep in mind that {documents=**} means that it includes all the subcollections inside the products collection.

The Local Emulator Suite

It would be helpful if Firebase's documentation emphasized how important it is to use this tool, especially for those who use Firebase Functions. However, it doesn't seem like the documentation talks about it very much. There are entries on their website related to the Emulator Suite, but it feels a little messy sometimes. It took me months to understand that I could take advantage of that Emulator Suite and how to use it properly.

This tool lets you test your projects on your own computer without affecting the real services. The local emulators support Firestore, Realtime Database, Functions, Storage, Authentication, and PubSubs.

Testing your security rules and storage rules with the local emulators before you publish them to Google Cloud can help you find problems early. Plus, it can help you save money because fewer deploys mean fewer charges. Keeping all your rules in one place also makes it easier to keep track of them.

You can see the official documentation to learn more about the Local Emulator Suite

Optimizations for performance and scalability

It might seem like a waste of time to focus on scalability and performance at the start of a small project. This is correct, in a way. Engineers have to avoid over-engineering. However, it is important to have a solid understanding of algorithms and logic so you are prepared when the project grows and performance becomes an issue. Otherwise, you may end up having to spend a lot of time and money fixing slow execution time or high memory consumption. To be efficient, you should learn techniques for writing more efficient Firebase Functions, like how to delete big collections or update many documents with complicated conditions.

How to delete large collections

Some collections can grow to millions of documents, and deleting documents inside of them can be a computation-intensive task. The examples below come from the official Firebase documentation. These show the recommended way of handling this type of task.

async function deleteCollection(db, collectionPath, batchSize) {
  const collectionRef = db.collection(collectionPath);
  const query = collectionRef.orderBy('__name__').limit(batchSize);   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve).catch(reject);
  });
} async function deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve) {
  const snapshot = await query.get();   const batchSize = snapshot.size;
  if (batchSize === 0) {
    // When there are no documents left, we are done
    resolve();
    return;
  }   // Delete documents in a batch
  const batch = db.batch();
  snapshot.docs.forEach((doc) => {
    batch.delete(doc.ref);
  });
  await batch.commit();   // Recurse on the next process tick, to avoid
  // exploding the stack.
  process.nextTick(() => {
    deleteQueryBatch(db, query, resolve);
  });
}

If you're working with big files and running into processing issues, one solution could be to give your function more resources, like extra RAM or a longer timeout.

exports.convertLargeFile = functions
    .runWith({
      // Ensure the function has enough memory and time
      // to process large files
      timeoutSeconds: 300,
      memory: "1GB",
    })
    .storage.object()
    .onFinalize((object) => {
      // Do some complicated things that take a lot of memory and time
    })

Enqueued Cloud Tasks

For time-consuming, resource-intensive, or bandwidth-limited tasks that need to run asynchronously, Google Cloud offers the possibility to enqueue functions with Cloud Tasks. You can control the number of attempts, backoff second, and the max of concurrent dispatches. The example below comes from the official documentation:

exports.backupApod = functions
    .runWith( {secrets: ["SOME_KEY"]})
    .tasks.taskQueue({
      retryConfig: {
        maxAttempts: 5,
        minBackoffSeconds: 60,
      },
      rateLimits: {
        maxConcurrentDispatches: 6,
      },
    }).onDispatch(async (data) => { //your complex task })

exports.enqueueBackupTasks = functions.https.onRequest(
async (_request, response) => {
  const queue = getFunctions().taskQueue("backupApod");
  const enqueues = [];
  for (let i = 0; i <= 10; i += 1) {
    // Enqueue each task with i*60 seconds day. Our task queue function
    // should process ~1 task/min.
    const scheduleDelaySeconds = i * 60
    enqueues.push(
        queue.enqueue(
          { id: `task-${i}` },
          {
            scheduleDelaySeconds,
            dispatchDeadlineSeconds: 60 * 5 // 5 minutes
          },
        ),
    );
  }
  await Promise.all(enqueues);
  response.sendStatus(200); });

Backups in Firebase

As far as I know, right now Google Cloud doesn't have a way to automatically backup Firestore. I use BackupFire to do it for me, but you can also write a solution using Firebase Functions. Just keep in mind that when you restore a backup, Firebase tries to merge it with the original database, not overwrite it. This can have important consequences. Consider this example:

There are two customers:

  • User A, uid: z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2
  • User B, uid: Zk5olEuvZ1WokRrCqIN5gGaIu353

User A gained a gift card or discount code and it was saved in a subcollection inside the user document.

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discountCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

{ code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: false
}

The app allows users to transfer codes to others. User A transferred the code to User B

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discountCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

{ code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true
}

However, a backup from the previous day had to be restored due to an emergency. Upon checking, there were inconsistencies in the database. Both User A and User B now have the same discount code.

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

{ code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: false
}

users/z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2/discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

{ code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true
}

To avoid these issues in Firestore, you can create a script that verifies the correct documents and removes the duplicates. Another solution is to design your database in a way that avoids documents that can be transferred, for example, with a collection in the root path of your database.

discoutCodes/dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2

{ code: 'dGn5OxSEPohqpZbIyLmCjqVEiwz2', expirationDate: '2023-01-18T18:34:33.565Z', isAGift: true createdBy: z1V5scLfVPX7YD8fRCFRhhRAwVN2 owner: Zk5olEuvZ1WokRrCqIN5gGaIu353
}

In this way, there is only one code available and transfer it to another user means just editing the owner field inside this document. Keep in mind that this is a specific problem related to the key-value internal structure of Firestore and may not occur in other databases.

Conclusions

In conclusion, it is important to consider scalability and performance efficiency in the early stages when designing a project, especially with Firebase; but without over-engineering. This can save time and resources in the long run. Additionally, it is essential to have a backup plan for Firestore, as restoring a backup can sometimes result in inconsistencies. To mitigate these issues, one can either use a third-party service or create their own solution based on Firebase Functions. It is also crucial to design the database in such a way as to avoid transferable subcollections that could lead to document duplications.

Bibliography

Backup Fire | 2022 | BackupFire Official Site

Firebase Documentation | 2022 | Emulator Suite

Firebase Documentation | 2022 | Security Rules Basics

Firebase Documentation | 2022 | Security Rules Getting Started

Google Developers | 2022 | Task Functions

Google Developers | 2022 | Manage Functions

Google Cloud | 2002 | Delete a Firestore collection
item.author: jodaut

item.title: 成为 ReactJS 开发者：基本技能和未来前景 | Becoming a ReactJS Developer: Essential Skills and Future Prospects
item.description:
介绍：
凭借其高需求和众多应用程序，ReactJS 在未来为开发人员提供了广阔的空间。 在本文中，我们将讨论 ReactJS 开发人员应具备的基本技能以及 ReactJS 的未来前景。

他们应该能够设计出既实用又美观的用户界面。

熟悉 SASS 或 LESS 等 CSS 预处理器也很有帮助。

他们还应该对软件开发原则和最佳实践有深刻的理解。

七、沟通技巧：
随着越来越多的组织采用 ReactJS，对熟练的 ReactJS 开发人员的需求只会继续增加。 以下是 ReactJS 的未来对开发人员来说前景广阔的几个原因：

这种广泛的采用意味着在未来几年将对 ReactJS 开发人员有强烈的需求。

随着跨平台开发的趋势越来越强烈，对精通 ReactJS 的开发人员的需求越来越大。 这种强大的社区支持意味着 ReactJS 很可能在未来继续发展壮大。

结论：
要成为一名成功的 ReactJS 开发人员，必须对 JavaScript 和 ReactJS 有扎实的理解，了解 UI 和 UX 设计、CSS 和其他相关技术、解决问题的能力以及出色的沟通技巧。 凭借这些技能，ReactJS 开发人员可以构建高质量、用户友好的应用程序，并为任何项目的成功做出贡献。



原文:

Introduction:
ReactJS is an open-source JavaScript library for building user interfaces and single-page applications. Developed by Facebook, ReactJS has become one of the most popular front-end development technologies. With its high demand and numerous applications, ReactJS has a vast scope for developers in the future. In this article, we will discuss the essential skills that a ReactJS developer should have and the future prospects of ReactJS.

Essential Skills for a ReactJS Developer:

1. Strong JavaScript Skills:
ReactJS is a JavaScript-based library, and it is crucial to have a solid understanding of JavaScript programming language to work with ReactJS. A ReactJS developer should be proficient in concepts such as ES6, JavaScript functions, arrays, objects, etc.

2. Knowledge of ReactJS Library:
A ReactJS developer should have a deep understanding of ReactJS and its core concepts, such as components, props, state, lifecycle methods, and hooks.

3. Understanding of UI and UX Design:
ReactJS developers should have a good understanding of UI and UX design principles. They should be able to design user interfaces that are both functional and aesthetically pleasing.

4. Knowledge of CSS:
ReactJS developers should have a good understanding of CSS and how to style ReactJS components. Familiarity with CSS preprocessors like SASS or LESS can also be helpful.

5. Familiarity with Other Tools and Technologies:
ReactJS developers should be familiar with other technologies used in front-end development, such as HTML, Git, npm, webpack, and others.

6. Problem-Solving Skills:
ReactJS developers should have excellent problem-solving skills and be able to find solutions to complex problems. They should also have a strong understanding of software development principles and best practices.

7. Communication Skills:
ReactJS developers should have strong communication skills and be able to effectively communicate with other team members, stakeholders, and clients.

Future of ReactJS:
ReactJS is a popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces, and its future looks bright for developers. As more and more organizations adopt ReactJS, the demand for skilled ReactJS developers will only continue to increase. Here are a few reasons why the future of ReactJS looks promising for developers:

1. Widespread adoption:
ReactJS has been adopted by many of the world's top companies, including Facebook, Airbnb, Netflix, and Dropbox. This widespread adoption means that there will be a strong demand for ReactJS developers in the coming years.

2. Growing interest in React Native:
ReactJS is the foundation for React Native, a popular platform for building native mobile apps. The popularity of React Native is growing, and this growth is driving demand for ReactJS developers who are proficient in both ReactJS and React Native.

3. Growing demand for cross-platform development:
With the growing trend of cross-platform development, the demand for developers who are proficient in ReactJS is increasing. ReactJS allows developers to build applications that can run on multiple platforms, making it a valuable skill for developers in the future.

4. Community support:
ReactJS has a strong and supportive community of developers who are constantly contributing new features and improvements. This strong community support means that ReactJS is likely to continue to evolve and grow in the future.

Conclusion:
ReactJS has a vast scope for developers in the future, and it is an excellent technology for front-end development. To be a successful ReactJS developer, it is essential to have a solid understanding of JavaScript and ReactJS, knowledge of UI and UX design, CSS, and other related technologies, problem-solving skills, and excellent communication skills. With these skills, ReactJS developers can build high-quality, user-friendly applications and contribute to the success of any project.

布基佐伊

时代教授 en la Escuela de Negocios Jon M. Huntsman de la Universidad Estatal de Utah en el momento de su muerte。

角色。 写下项目和元数据，写下 resultados que desea y los pasos que debe seguir para llegar allí。

优先考虑您的任务或任务，而不是进入其他问题。 没有必要竞争 o pelear con otros para obtener su porción del pastel，piense que siempre hay suficiente para todos y trabajar para ayudar a los demás en lugar de solo a usted mismo。 请考虑让其他人感到高兴，但请考虑让自己感到幸福，并在解决方案中寻求帮助。 图书细节

原文:

bookeyzoe

Posted on Feb 8

Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva : revisión resumida y conclusiones

Esta es una revisión resumida de Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva que contiene detalles clave sobre el libro.

¿De qué trata " Los 7 Hábitos de la Gente Altamente Efectiva "?
Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva es un libro de negocios y autoayuda. El autor presenta un enfoque para ser efectivo en el logro de metas al alinearse con lo que él llama los principios del "norte verdadero". Presenta sus enseñanzas en una serie de hábitos, que se manifiestan como una progresión desde la dependencia, pasando por la independencia, hasta la interdependencia.

Resumen de los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva

¿Quién es el autor de Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva? Stephen Richards Covey fue un educador, autor, hombre de negocios y orador principal estadounidense. La revista Time lo nombró una de las 25 personas más influyentes. Era profesor en la Escuela de Negocios Jon M. Huntsman de la Universidad Estatal de Utah en el momento de su muerte.

[ Cita favorita ]: “No es lo que nos pasa, sino nuestra respuesta a lo que nos pasa lo que nos duele”.

¿Cuáles son los puntos clave de Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva ?
Punto #1 Localice su posición actual
¿Cuántas veces has deseado tener un mapa para navegar la vida? Al prestar atención a sus paradigmas (un conjunto distinto de comportamientos o patrones de pensamiento: cómo reacciona ante las cosas), puede identificar dónde se encuentra en su vida y qué giros necesita hacer para llegar a su destino final de ser un altamente eficaz. persona. Cuando sus paradigmas son reconocidos, monitoreados y luego cambiados, se realizan cambios duraderos en su carácter que anteriormente lo habían estado frenando. A veces, comprender por qué una persona está reaccionando de cierta manera es suficiente para mejorar sus propios pensamientos y respuestas, pero otras veces se necesita más trabajo interno para que deje de reaccionar automáticamente, es decir, arremeter contra un ser querido y, en cambio, elija influir de manera proactiva. la situación actuando con amor, compasión y comprensión en lugar de con ira. Las personas reactivas tienden a preocuparse por las cosas que no pueden cambiar, mientras que las personas proactivas eligen trabajar solo en las cosas sobre las que pueden hacer algo. Si se da cuenta de que es reactivo, plantéese el desafío de concentrarse en las soluciones en lugar de preocuparse o acusar a los demás cuando surjan problemas y desafíos.

Punto n.° 2 Planifique y visualice Tienes que empezar con el final en mente. No se subiría a su automóvil con la expectativa de llegar a un nuevo lugar en un momento determinado sin saber cuánto tiempo le llevará el viaje o qué ruta tomar, pero en la vida, a menudo comenzamos las cosas sin tener ningún plan y, a menudo, sin un destino final claro en mente. Si piensas en tu mortalidad te darás cuenta de lo que es importante para ti y lo que no lo es. ¿Qué quieres lograr con tu vida? ¿Que quieres que de ti sea recordado? Puede convertir estos pensamientos en una declaración de misión para su vida para darle la seguridad de que tiene un plan para lo que quiere y hacia dónde se dirige en la vida. Escriba sus proyectos y metas, escriba los resultados que desea y los pasos que debe seguir para llegar allí.

Prioriza tu objetivo o misión para que no quede enterrado bajo los problemas cotidianos. Puede crear una lista de prioridades clasificando las tareas según su importancia y urgencia. Cree una matriz de 2x2 con 4 cuadrantes. El cuadrante 1 es para las tareas urgentes que deben abordarse de inmediato. El cuadrante 2 es para tareas que son importantes pero no urgentes. El cuadrante 3 es para tareas que son urgentes pero no importantes, como un correo electrónico que hace ping en su bandeja de entrada y necesita una respuesta mientras está trabajando en otra cosa. El cuadrante 4 son todas las tareas que no son ni urgentes ni importantes, las cosas que son una pérdida de tiempo. El cuadrante 2 es el área en la que necesita trabajar: debe ser la lista de cosas que mejorarán enormemente su vida. Probablemente descubrirá que es esta área la que actualmente más descuida, ya sea posponiendo las cosas para otro momento o distrayéndose con cosas de los cuadrantes 3 y 4.

Punto #3 Piense en positivo y trate a las personas con respeto.
Debe pensar en las situaciones como ganar-ganar, no ganar-perder. No necesita competir o pelear con otros para obtener su porción del pastel, piense que siempre hay suficiente para todos y trabajar para ayudar a los demás en lugar de solo a usted mismo. Sepa que al operar desde una perspectiva de ganar-perder muchas veces ambos pierden y alguien más se queda con el pastel. Considere con qué sería feliz la otra persona mientras también considera su propia felicidad y vea dónde se puede encontrar una solución. Asegúrate de escuchar a la otra persona correctamente, cuestionando para que entiendas completamente antes de entrometerte y decirle lo que quieres, lo que piensas y lo que sientes.

Detalles del libro

  • Longitud de impresión: 372 páginas
  • Audiolibro: 13 horas y 4 minutos
  • Género: Autoayuda, No ficción, Negocios, Desarrollo personal, Psicología

¿Cuáles son los capítulos de Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva ?
Capítulo uno: de adentro hacia afuera Capítulo dos: los 7 hábitos: una descripción general Capítulo tres: hábito 1: ser proactivo Capítulo cuatro: hábito 2: comenzar con el final en mente Capítulo cinco: hábito 3: poner primero lo primero Capítulo seis: hábito 4 - Piense en ganar/ganar Capítulo siete - Hábito 5 - Buscar primero comprender, luego ser comprendido Capítulo ocho - Hábito 6 - Sinergizar Capítulo nueve - Hábito 7 - Afilar la sierra

¿Cuál es una buena cita de Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva ?
“Muéstrenme a alguien que sea lo suficientemente humilde como para aceptar y asumir la responsabilidad de sus circunstancias y lo suficientemente valiente como para tomar cualquier iniciativa que sea necesaria para abrirse camino creativamente a través o alrededor de estos desafíos, y les mostraré el poder supremo de elección."

¿Qué dicen los críticos?
Esto es lo que uno de los destacados críticos dijo sobre el libro: “Ninguna persona dura para siempre, pero los libros y las ideas pueden perdurar. La vida de Stephen R. Covey está acabada, pero su obra no. Continúa, aquí mismo, en este libro, tan vivo hoy como cuando se escribió por primera vez”. —Jim Collins

¿Cuáles son Los 7 hábitos de la gente altamente efectiva ?

  • Los tres primeros hábitos rodean el paso de la dependencia a la independencia (es decir, el autodominio):

Hábito 1: "Sé proactivo": la proactividad se trata de asumir la responsabilidad de la propia reacción, tomar la iniciativa para responder positivamente y mejorar la situación.

Hábito 2: "Empezar con el fin en mente" - Si el hábito 1 aconseja cambiar de vida para actuar y ser proactivo, el hábito 2 aconseja que "el programador eres tú". El segundo hábito se trata de imaginar lo que uno quiere en el futuro (una declaración de misión personal) para que uno pueda trabajar y planificar para lograrlo. Para ser eficaz, es necesario actuar sobre la base de principios y revisar constantemente las declaraciones de misión. El autor nos anima a preguntarnos: ¿Eres, en este momento, quien quieres ser? ¿Qué tienes que decir sobre ti? Como quieres ser recordado?

Hábito 3: "Poner primero lo primero" - Si el hábito 2 advierte que "usted es el programador", el hábito 3 aconseja: "escriba el programa, conviértase en un líder". El autor habla de lo importante versus lo urgente. Se debe dar prioridad en el siguiente orden: Cuadrante I. Urgente e importante (Hacer) – plazos y crisis importantes. Cuadrante II. No urgente pero importante (Plan) – desarrollo a largo plazo. Cuadrante III. Urgente pero no importante (Delegado) – distracciones con plazos Cuadrante IV. No urgente y no importante (Eliminar) – distracciones frívolas

  • Los siguientes tres hábitos hablan de interdependencia (es decir, trabajar con otros):

Hábito 4: "Piense en ganar-ganar" - Busque soluciones beneficiosas para ambas partes en sus relaciones, dice el autor. Valorar y respetar a las personas al buscar una "ganancia" para todos es, en última instancia, una mejor resolución a largo plazo que si solo una persona en la situación se hubiera salido con la suya.

Hábito 5: "Busca primero comprender, luego ser comprendido" - Usa la escucha empática para comprender genuinamente a una persona, lo que la obliga a corresponder a la escucha y tener una mente abierta para ser influenciada. Esto crea una atmósfera de cuidado y resolución positiva de problemas.

Hábito 6: "Sinergizar": combinar las fortalezas de las personas a través del trabajo en equipo positivo, para lograr objetivos que nadie podría haber logrado solo, exhorta Covey.

  • El último hábito es el de la mejora continua tanto en el ámbito personal como en el interpersonal.

Hábito 7: "Afilar la sierra": Covey dice que uno debe equilibrar y renovar los recursos, la energía y la salud de uno para crear un estilo de vida sostenible, efectivo y a largo plazo. Él enfatiza principalmente el ejercicio para la renovación física, la buena oración y la buena lectura para la renovación mental. También menciona el servicio a la sociedad para la renovación espiritual.

¿Qué es el modelo de "espiral ascendente"?
A través de la conciencia, junto con un progreso significativo y constante, una espiral ascendente dará como resultado el crecimiento, el cambio y la mejora constante. El modelo de espiral ascendente consta de tres partes: aprender, comprometerse, hacer. Según Covey, hay que educar cada vez más la conciencia para crecer y desarrollarse en la espiral ascendente.

¿Qué es una "Mentalidad de Abundancia"? La mentalidad de abundancia (o mentalidad de abundancia) es una forma de pensar en la que una persona cree que hay suficientes recursos y éxitos para compartir con los demás. Lo contrasta con la mentalidad de escasez (es decir, competencia destructiva e innecesaria), que se basa en la idea de que si alguien más gana o tiene éxito en una situación, significa que "tú pierdes", porque no estás considerando la posibilidad de todos. las partes "ganan" de una forma u otra en una situación dada.

Las personas que tienen una mentalidad de abundancia rechazan la noción de los juegos de suma cero y pueden celebrar el éxito de los demás, en lugar de sentirse amenazados por ellos. El autor sostiene que la mentalidad de abundancia surge de tener una alta autoestima y seguridad y conduce a la participación en las ganancias, el reconocimiento y la responsabilidad.

item.title: 如何激活休眠账户？ | How to activate dormant account?
item.description:
银行业

发表于 2 月 8 日

休眠账户是指长期未使用的账户，在大多数情况下，银行认为其不活跃。 在本文中，我们将讨论如何激活休眠帐户、您需要执行的步骤以及您需要提供的信息。

什么是休眠账户？

休眠帐户是指在指定期间内未使用的帐户，通常为一到三年。 此期间可能因银行和国家/地区而异。 当账户处于休眠状态时，银行通常会停止发送报表，如果没有活动，则该账户可能被视为不活跃。

休眠的原因

帐户休眠的原因有很多。 一些最常见的原因包括：

  • 帐户持有人已死亡
  • 帐户持有人已搬家且未提供转发地址
  • 账户持有人有多个账户，忘记了休眠账户
  • 由于财务状况的变化，账户持有人已经很长时间没有使用该账户

如何激活休眠帐户

要激活休眠帐户，您需要执行以下步骤：

  • 联系银行：您可以亲自、通过电话或电子邮件联系银行。 最好致电银行并与客户服务代表交谈，了解您需要采取哪些步骤来激活您的账户。
  • 提供身份证明：您需要向银行提供身份证明以证明您是账户持有人。 这可以是政府签发的身份证件，例如驾照或护照。
  • 提供地址证明：您还需要向银行提供您当前地址的证明，例如水电费账单或银行对账单。
  • 更新您的联系信息：您还应该更新您与银行的联系信息，包括您的邮寄地址、电子邮件地址和电话号码。 这将确保您收到来自银行的任何未来报表或通讯。
  • 签署一张新的签名卡：在大多数情况下，您需要签署一张新的签名卡来激活您的休眠账户。 此卡将使用您当前的签名更新银行的记录，这些记录将用于验证未来的交易。

所需文件

要激活休眠帐户，您可能需要提供以下文件：

  • 身份证明：可以是政府签发的身份证件，例如驾照或护照。
  • 地址证明：这可以是不到三个月的公用事业账单或银行对账单的形式。
  • 签名卡：这是一张您签名以使用您当前的签名更新银行记录的卡。

激活休眠账户的费用

在某些情况下，激活休眠帐户可能会产生相关费用。 这些费用可能包括账户重新激活费、年费以及制作报表或其他文件副本的费用。 在激活您的帐户之前，您应该向银行询问可能适用的任何费用。

结论

激活休眠账户是一个简单的过程，需要您向银行提供身份证明、地址证明和更新的联系信息。 在大多数情况下，您还需要签署一张新的签名卡以更新银行的记录。 在激活您的帐户之前，您应该向银行询问可能与该过程相关的任何费用。

原文:

banking kaise

Posted on Feb 8

A dormant account is one that has not been used for a long time, and in most cases, the bank considers it inactive. In this article, we will discuss how to activate a dormant account, the steps you need to take, and the information you need to provide.

What is a Dormant Account?

A dormant account is one that has not been used for a specified period, which is usually between one and three years. This period may vary from bank to bank and country to country. When an account is dormant, the bank will typically stop sending statements, and if there is no activity, the account may be considered inactive.

Reasons for Dormancy

There can be many reasons why an account becomes dormant. Some of the most common reasons include:

  • The account holder has died
  • The account holder has moved and has not provided a forwarding address
  • The account holder has multiple accounts and has forgotten about the dormant account
  • The account holder has not used the account for a long time due to a change in financial circumstances

How to Activate a Dormant Account

To activate a dormant account, you need to take the following steps:

  • Contact the bank: You can contact the bank either in person, by phone, or by email. It is best to call the bank and speak to a customer service representative to find out what steps you need to take to activate your account.
  • Provide identification: You will need to provide the bank with identification to prove that you are the account holder. This can be in the form of a government-issued ID, such as a driver's license or passport.
  • Provide proof of address: You will also need to provide the bank with proof of your current address, such as a utility bill or bank statement.
  • Update your contact information: You should also update your contact information with the bank, including your mailing address, email address, and phone number. This will ensure that you receive any future statements or communications from the bank.
  • Sign a new signature card: In most cases, you will need to sign a new signature card to activate your dormant account. This card will update the bank's records with your current signature, which will be used to verify future transactions.

Documentation Required

To activate a dormant account, you may need to provide the following documentation:

  • Proof of identification: This can be in the form of a government-issued ID, such as a driver's license or passport.
  • Proof of address: This can be in the form of a utility bill or bank statement that is less than three months old.
  • Signature card: This is a card that you sign to update the bank's records with your current signature.

Fees for Activating a Dormant Account

In some cases, there may be fees associated with activating a dormant account. These fees may include account reactivation fees, annual fees, and fees for producing copies of statements or other documents. Before activating your account, you should ask the bank about any fees that may be applicable.

Conclusion

Activating a dormant account is a straightforward process that requires you to provide the bank with identification, proof of address, and updated contact information. In most cases, you will also need to sign a new signature card to update the bank's records. Before activating your account, you should ask the bank about any fees that may be associated with the process.

原文:

I built a multilingual ai chatbot powered by GPT-3. It is built using React and Next.js and is available at giti.ai. It supports over 100+ languages and allows users to interact with the GPT model in their native language.

Category Submission:

Artificial Intelligence, ChatGPT, Next.js, React

App Link

www.giti.ai

Screenshots


Giti.ai Homepage


English


Hindi


Chinese


Russian


Deutsch

Description

Giti.ai is a multilingual AI-powered chatbot utilizing the latest GPT-3 model. Our ChatGPT supports over 100 languages and allows for natural, smooth conversations in your native language. With Giti.ai, you can talk to an intelligent chatbot that understands your native language.

Background

OpenAI's ChatGPT is mainly trained on English and a few other popular languages and performs poorly in languages like Arabic, Persian, Hebrew, and etc.
ChatGPT is an incredible tool and my goal was to enable people regardless of their English language knowledge level to have access to this awesome tool.

How I built it

I built it using next.js, aws, gcp, and gpt-3.

Features

The chatbot provides a number of features including:

  • Natural language understanding
  • Multilingual support for over 100 languages
  • Ability to customize responses and create custom conversations
  • Ability to provide personalized answers
  • Ability to learn from conversations and improve over time.
  • Supported languages are: English, 简体中文, हिन्दी, Español, Français, Deutsch, العربية, Русский, 日本語, Italiano, বাংলা, Português, اردو, ਪੰਜਾਬੀ, Bahasa Indonesia, मराठी, Türkçe, Việt Nam, فارسی, Polski, Afrikaans, Shqip, አማርኛ, Հայերեն, অসমীয়া, aymar aru, Azərbaycan dili, bamanankan, Euskara, Беларуская, भोजपुरी, Bosanski, български, Català, Cebuano, Corsu, Hrvatski, Čeština, Dansk, ދިވެހި, डोगरी, Nederlands, Esperanto, Eesti, Eʋegbe, Filipino, Suomi, Frysk, Galego, ქართული, Ελληνικά, Avañe'ẽ, ગુજરાતી, Kreyòl ayisyen, هَوُسَ, ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi, עברית, Hmoob, Magyar, Íslenska, Igbo, Ilokano, Gaeilge, Basa Jawa, ಕನ್ನಡ, қазақ тілі, ភាសាខ្មែរ, Ikinyarwanda, कोंकणी, 한국어, Kriyòl, Kurdî, کوردی, Кыргызча, ພາສາລາວ, Latine, Latviešu, Lingála, Lietuvių, Luganda, Lëtzebuergesch, Македонски, मैथिली, Malagasy, Bahasa Melayu, മലയാളം, Malti, Māori, Mizo, Монгол, नेपाली, Norsk, Chichewa, ଓଡ଼ିଆ, Oromoo, پښتو, Runasimi, Română, Gagana Samoa, संस्कृतम्, Gàidhlig, Sesotho sa Leboa, Српски, Sesotho, Shona, سنڌي, සිංහල, Slovenčina, Slovenščina, Af-Soomaali, Basa Sunda, Kiswahili, Svenska, Тоҷикӣ, தமிழ், Татарча, తెలుగు, ไทย, ትግርኛ, Xitsonga, Türkmençe, Twi, Українська, ئۇيغۇرچە, O'zbekcha, Cymraeg, isiXhosa, ייִדיש, Yorùbá, isiZulu

item.title: 如何开始在 Hackerrank 中解决问题。 | How to start problem solving in Hackerrank.
item.description:
沙米尔·里多

发表于 2 月 8 日

如果您和我一样，就会喜欢挑战。 但有时，当您遇到一个特别困难的问题时，袖手旁观一些技巧可能会有所帮助。 在这篇博文中，我将与您分享我在 Hackerrank 中解决问题的一些重要技巧。

  1. 仔细阅读问题。

这听起来似乎很容易，但在开始编码之前确保您理解问题是非常重要的。 有时措辞可能会令人困惑，或者您可能会误解说明。 如果您不确定问题在问什么，最好寻求澄清，而不是开始编码却弄错了。

  1. 将问题分解成更小的部分。

阅读并理解问题后，将其分解为更小的子问题会很有帮助。 这将使您更容易了解如何解决问题和编写代码。

  1. 尝试不同的方法。

因此，如果您遇到困难，尝试不同的方法可能会有所帮助。 有时蛮力方法会奏效，而有时您可能需要更有创意。

  1. 不要害怕寻求帮助。

如果你真的被困住了，不要害怕寻求帮助。 承认您需要一些帮助并不可耻。 您可以在 Hackerrank 论坛上寻求帮助，甚至可以直接联系问题的作者。

解决 Hackerrank 问题可能很困难，但坚持不懈很重要。 如果您遇到问题，请休息一下，稍后再回来处理。 有时，您所需要的只是一个全新的视角。

我希望这些提示对您有所帮助。 编码愉快！

原文:

Shahmir Riddo

Posted on Feb 8

If you're anything like me, you love a good challenge. But sometimes, when you're stuck on a particularly difficult problem, it can be helpful to have a few tricks up your sleeve. In this blog post, I'm going to share with you some of my top tips for solving problems in Hackerrank.

  1. Read the problem carefully.

This may sound like a no-brainer, but it's really important to make sure you understand the problem before you start coding. Sometimes the wording can be confusing, or you might misinterpret the instructions. If you're not sure what the problem is asking, it's better to ask for clarification than to start coding and get it wrong.

  1. Break the problem down into smaller parts.

Once you've read and understood the problem, it can be helpful to break it down into smaller sub-problems. This will make it easier to see how to approach the problem and write the code.

  1. Experiment with different approaches.

Hackerrank problems often have more than one correct solution. So if you're stuck, it can be helpful to try a different approach. Sometimes the brute-force approach will work, while other times you might need to get a bit more creative.

  1. Don't be afraid to ask for help.

If you're really stuck, don't be afraid to ask for help. There's no shame in admitting that you need some assistance. You can ask for help on the Hackerrank forums, or even reach out to the problem's author directly.

Solving Hackerrank problems can be tough, but it's important to persevere. If you get stuck on a problem, take a break and come back to it later. Sometimes all you need is a fresh perspective.

I hope these tips have been helpful. Happy coding!

item.title: [C#] log4net 用法 | [C#] log4net usage
item.description:
这就是在.Net 环境下使用log4net 处理日志记录的方法。

  1. 在Visual Studio的NuGet包管理中搜索log4net并安装。

  2. 安装完成后，log4net 添加到引用中。

  3. 创建一个新的配置文件并添加它。

 <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <log4net> <root> <level value="ALL" /> <appender-ref ref="RollingFileAppender" /> </root> <appender name="RollingFileAppender" type="log4net.Appender.RollingFileAppender"> <!--<file type="log4net.Util.PatternString" value="log-%property{processid}-%date{yyyy-MM-dd}.txt" />--> <file value="Log\common.log" /> <appendToFile value="true" /> <rollingStyle value="Size" /> <maxSizeRollBackups value="5" /> <maximumFileSize value="10MB" /> <staticLogFileName value="true" /> <layout type="log4net.Layout.PatternLayout"> <conversionPattern value="%date [%thread] %-5level %logger [%ndc] - %message%newline" /> </layout> </appender> </log4net>

有关如何创建配置文件的详细信息，请参见此处。

  1. 配置文件在分发程序时可能作为一个单独的文件存在，但在这里，为了将其包含在程序集中，在属性窗口中将构建操作指定为“嵌入资源”。

  2. 现在我们实际上是在我们想要留下日志的地方进行日志处理。

 namespace log4net_test { internal class Program { private static readonly ILog log = LogManager.GetLogger(typeof(Program)); static void Main(string[] args) { var assembly = typeof(Program).Assembly; using (var stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream("log4net_test.config.log4net.config")) { log4net.Config.XmlConfigurator.Configure(stream); } log.Info("Application started"); Console.WriteLine("Press Enter to exit"); Console.ReadLine(); log.Info("Application ended"); } } }

此处，作为嵌入式资源的配置文件作为 GetManifestResourceStream() 方法的参数值传递。 在示例的情况下，由于创建了“config”文件夹并且配置文件位于其下，因此文件夹名称为“.”。 您需要将它放在命名空间和文件名之间，如上所示。

如果在构建后运行该程序，将创建在配置文件 Log\common_log 中指定的日志文件，并且该日志将保留。

原文:

This is how to use log4net to process logging in .Net environment.

  1. Search for log4net in Visual Studio's NuGet package management and install it.

  2. When the installation is complete, log4net is added to the references.

  3. Create a new configuration file and add it.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<log4net>
  <root>
    <level value="ALL" />
    <appender-ref ref="RollingFileAppender" />
  </root>
  <appender name="RollingFileAppender" type="log4net.Appender.RollingFileAppender">
    <!--<file type="log4net.Util.PatternString" value="log-%property{processid}-%date{yyyy-MM-dd}.txt" />-->
    <file value="Log\common.log" />
    <appendToFile value="true" />
    <rollingStyle value="Size" />
    <maxSizeRollBackups value="5" />
    <maximumFileSize value="10MB" />
    <staticLogFileName value="true" />
    <layout type="log4net.Layout.PatternLayout">
      <conversionPattern value="%date [%thread] %-5level %logger [%ndc] - %message%newline" />
    </layout>
  </appender>
</log4net>

For details on how to create the configuration file, see here.

  1. The configuration file may exist as a separate file when distributing the program, but here, in order to include it in the assembly, designate the build operation as 'embedded resource' in the property window.

  2. Now we actually do log processing where we want to leave logs.

namespace log4net_test
{
    internal class Program
    {
        private static readonly ILog log = LogManager.GetLogger(typeof(Program));

        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            var assembly = typeof(Program).Assembly;
            using (var stream = assembly.GetManifestResourceStream("log4net_test.config.log4net.config"))
            {
                log4net.Config.XmlConfigurator.Configure(stream);
            }

            log.Info("Application started");
            Console.WriteLine("Press Enter to exit");
            Console.ReadLine();
            log.Info("Application ended");
        }
    }
}

Here, the configuration file as an embedded resource is passed as an argument value of the GetManifestResourceStream() method. In the case of the example, since the 'config' folder was created and the configuration file was placed under it, the folder name was '.' You need to put it between the namespace and the file name as above.

If you run the program after building, the log file specified in the configuration file, Log\common_log, will be created and the log will remain.
